burn.def: 燃燒、燒傷、發熱、灼熱、燒傷的人物、燃燒地 (燒)
burn.col: burn down, burn out, burn the midnight oil (熬夜), severe burn
burn.ex: The fire started to burn more brightly.

burned/burnt.def: 燒焦的、燒傷的、燒毀的人 (或物)
burned/burnt.ex: He applied ointment to his sunburnt skin.

burner.def: 爐嘴、燃燒器、燒東西的人 (單數)
burner.ex: Don't touch the stove; the burner is still hot.

burning.def: 燃燒的、灼熱的、迫切的、燃燒的人 (或物) (燒)
burning.ex: Unemployment is a burning issue in the region.

burnish.def: 擦亮、磨光、磨光的人 (或物)、磨光地 (亮)
burnish.ex: The metal was burnished until it shone like a mirror.

burnt.ex: The walls were painted a rich burnt orange color.

burp.def: 打嗝、使打嗝、打嗝的人 (或物)、打嗝地 (嗝)
burp.ex: The baby gave a giant burp after finishing his bottle.

burr/bur.def: (植物)刺果、(鑽)毛刺、去除毛刺的人、刺果地 (刺)
burr/bur.ex: The machine helps to remove the burr from metal edges.

burrito.ex: He ordered a spicy beef burrito for lunch.

burrow.def: 洞穴、地道、鑽洞、翻找、地道的人物、在地道地 (洞)
burrow.ex: The mole started to burrow into the garden soil.

bursa.ex: He has a swollen bursa in his shoulder joint.

bursar.ex: The university bursar is responsible for financial matters.

bursary.def: 獎學金、助學金、司庫辦公室 (單數)
bursary.ex: She was awarded an educational bursary to study abroad.

burst.def: 爆裂、爆發、炸開、突發、爆裂的人 (或物)、爆裂地 (爆)
burst.col: burst into tears, burst out laughing, star burst, cloud burst (大暴雨)
burst.ex: The balloon burst with a loud bang.

bursting.def: 爆滿的、充滿的的人 (或物) (滿)
bursting.ex: The stadium was bursting with excited football fans.

bury.def: 埋葬、掩埋、隱藏、埋葬的人 (或物)、埋葬地 (葬)
bury.col: bury the hatchet (和解), bury one's head in the sand (逃避現實)
bury.ex: They decided to bury the ancient jar in the garden.

bus.def: 公車、巴士、乘公車、公車的人物、公車地 (公車)
bus.col: bus stop, bus lane, bus terminal, miss the bus
bus.ex: I take the bus to work every morning.

busboy.ex: The busy busboy quickly cleared the dirty table.

busby.ex: The guardsman wore a tall busby for the parade.

bush.def: 灌木、矮樹叢、(俚)荒野、長滿灌木的人物、灌木地 (灌木)
bush.col: beat around the bush (轉彎抹角), bushfire, blackberry bush
bush.ex: A rabbit ran into the thick green bush.

bushel.def: 蒲式耳(容量)、大量的人 (或物) (蒲)
bushel.ex: They harvested over a bushel of fresh apples.

bushido.ex: The samurais lived according to the code of bushido.

bushman.ex: The Kalahari bushmen are famous for their tracking skills.

bushy.def: 灌木茂密的、(毛髮)濃密的的人 (或物)
bushy.ex: The squirrel has a long, bushy tail.

busily.ex: Everyone was working busily to meet the deadline.

business.def: 商業、生意、事情、職責、商業的人物、商業地 (商)
business.col: on business, do business with, none of your business (不關你的事), business hours
business.ex: He is in town on business for a few days.

businesslike.ex: She handled the complex matter in a very businesslike manner.

businessman.ex: The successful businessman owns several local companies.

businesswoman.ex: She is an ambitious businesswoman who runs a tech firm.

busk.def: 街頭談唱、街頭表演的人物、街頭地 (演)
busk.ex: He used to busk in the subway to earn some extra cash.

busker.ex: The talented busker played his guitar for the commuters.

busking.def: 街頭表演、街頭藝人的活動的人 (或物) (演)
busking.ex: Busking is allowed in certain areas of the city center.

buss.def: (舊)親吻、親吻的人 (或物)、親吻地 (吻)
buss.ex: He gave his grandmother a loud buss on the cheek.

bust.def: 胸像、(俚)破產、搜捕、打破、半身、失敗的人、破產地 (破)
bust.col: bust-up (劇烈爭吵), drug bust, go bust, bust a gut (竭盡全力)
bust.ex: The company went bust after only six months of trading.

busted.def: (俚)當場被抓、破產的、壞掉的人的人 (或物)
busted.ex: The teenagers got busted for smoking in the toilets.
